calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JASON A. BEZIS, Esq. of Law Offices of Jason A. Bezis (Tel: 925-708-7073) for petitioners/plaintiffs;
Atty. ADAM W. HOFMANN, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett LLP (Tel: 415-777-3200) for respondent/defendant Association of Bay Area Governments.

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS NEW LIVABLE CALIFORNIA AND COMMUNITY VENTURE PARTNERS, INC.'s Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees And Costs.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioners' "motion for award of reasonable attorney fees and costs" is granted in the amounts of $57,975 and $1,808 respectively.

In a stipulated judgment, respondent Association of Bay Area Governments (ABAG) admitted that it violated a key provision of California's key sunshine law - the Brown Act (Govt. Code Sec. 54953(c)(2)) - in 2019 when it "failed to report out the individual votes of each member of the ABAG Board." ABAG further stipulated that it would obey the law "in the future." Legislative history indicates, in fact, that Sec. 54953(c)(2) was passed in 2013 in part because "ABAG a large government agency governed by the Brown Act, failed to conduct either a roll call vote or a specific tally and report the votes of each member of the board."

I have ruled on many attorney fee motions and find this one reasonable. (See Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49.) One lawyer did the lion's share of the work and review of his time records shows he did it efficiently. A $500 hourly rate for a skilled lawyer with more than 18 years' experience is modest by our legal community's standards. The requests for judicial notice are granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Bezis, Esq. 925-708-7073 Petitioner/Plaintiff.
David Casarrubias, Esq. 415-995-5893 for Respondent/Defendant Association of Bay Area Governments.

RULING - A R G U E D. Petitioner argues catalyst theory. Respondent responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER NEW LIVABLE CALIFORNIA, COMMUNITY VENTURE PARTNERS, INC.'s Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees (CCP section 1021.5; GC section 54960.5)

Petitioners/Plaintiffs' motion for award of reasonable attorney fees is denied. On September 10, 2019, after sustaining without leave to amend Defendants' demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate and complaint for injunctive and declaratory relief, the Court entered judgment against Petitioners, and directed that they "shall take nothing by reason of their complaint" and that the action be dismissed with prejudice. Petitioners are not entitled to attorneys' fees under the Ralph M. Brown Act. Government Code section 54960.5 authorizes a court to award reasonable attorney fees to the plaintiff only "where it is found that a legislative body of the local agency has violated this chapter" or an action is dismissed because a legislative body has provided an unconditional commitment pursuant to section 54960.2(c)(1). The Court made no finding that Respondent violated the Brown Act. Further, Petitioners concede that Respondent did not provide an unconditional commitment to cease and desist from past alleged violations within the meaning of the statute, and the verbal statement by Respondent's Vice President did not meet any of the statutory requirements for such a commitment. (See Gov. Code 54960.2(c)(1), (2) [commitment must be made in writing within 30 days of receiving cease and desist letter, must state that commitment may be rescinded only by majority vote, and shall be approved by the legislative body in open session as a separate item of business].)

Petitioners' alternative request for attorneys' fees under section 1021.5 of the Code of Civil Procedure is ill-founded. Petitioners were not the "successful party," as they did not prevail on any issue in the litigation, judgment was entered against them, and they have appealed. (See Riverwatch v. County of San Diego Dept. of Environmental Health (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 768, 783 [the successful party under section 1021.5 is generally the "prevailing party," that is, the party that "succeeds on any significant issue in litigation which achieves some of the benefit the parties sought in bringing suit."].) As for Petitioners' catalyst theory, the Brown Act prescribes the conditions under which a court may award attorneys' fees to a party where the legislative body makes an unconditional commitment to cease and desist from an alleged violation of the Act. As the more specific statute, it controls. (See State Dept. of Public Health v. Superior Court (2015) 60 Cal.4th 940, 960-961.) In any event, Petitioners have not shown that their lawsuit-which was filed two weeks after the verbal statement that they now claim constituted a commitment-was a catalyst motivating Respondent to provide the primary relief sought in their complaint. (See Coalition for a Sustainable Future in Yucaipa v. City of Yucaipa (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 513, 521 ["At the very least, a plaintiff must establish the precise factual/legal condition that it sought to change or affect as a prerequisite for establishing the catalytic effect of its lawsuit."].) Petitioners did not achieve the primary relief they sought, which was to set aside Respondent's actions approving the CASA Compact. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Petitioner New Livable California, Community Venture Partners, Inc. Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees (Tentative Ruling Only, Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Off Calendar

Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Continued To Dec-17-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Costs
Off Calendar Nov-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Bezis, Esq. 925-708-7073 for Plaintiff.
David Casarrubias, Esq. 415-777-3200, Adam Hofmann, Esq. 916-442-3333 of Hanson Bridgett for Defendant ASSOCIATION OF BAY AREA GOVERNMENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues CCP 1032. Defendant argues primary relief. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ASSOCIATION OF BAY AREA GOVERNMENTS' Motion To Strike Costs.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Respondent Association of Bay Area Governments' motion to strike the memorandum of costs filed by Petitioners New Livable California and Community Venture Partners, Inc. is granted. On September 10, 2019, the Court entered judgment against Petitioners, and directed that they "shall take nothing by reason of their complaint" and that the action be dismissed with prejudice. Petitioners therefore are not prevailing parties entitled to recover costs under Code Civ. Proc., 1032. Nor are they entitled to costs under the Ralph M. Brown Act. Government Code section 54960.5 authorizes a court to award costs under Gov. Code 54960.2 only "where it is found that a legislative body of the local agency has violated this chapter" or an action is dismissed because a legislative body has provided an unconditional commitment pursuant to section 54960.2(c)(1). The court made no finding that Respondent violated the Brown Act, and Petitioners themselves deny that Respondent provided an unconditional commitment to cease and desist from past actions within the meaning of the statute. (See Opp. at 6.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
